cps/scopes

  Source   Edit

Scopes are used to keep track of control-flow targets; calls, breaks, continues, returns, flow-through, that sort of thing.

Imports

spec

Types

Scope = ref object
  parent*: NimNode
  kind*: NimNodeKind
  node*: NimNode
  label*: NimNode
  name*: NimNode
  goto*: Scope
  brake*: Scope
  scope*: Scope
  Source   Edit 
Scopes = seq[Scope]
  Source   Edit

Procs

func isNil(scope: Scope): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
true if the scope scope is undefined   Source   Edit 
proc kind(scope: Scope): NimNodeKind {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
what kind of ast created the scope?   Source   Edit 
func isEmpty(scope: Scope): bool {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
true if the scope scope is Empty or Nil   Source   Edit 
proc `$`(scope: Scope): string {...}{.raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc newScope(parent: Scope = nil): Scope {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
sentinel value for searches, etc.   Source   Edit 
proc next(ns: Scopes): Scope {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
read the next call off the stack   Source   Edit 
proc last(ns: Scopes): Scope {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
query the last loop in the stack   Source   Edit 
proc breakName(n: NimNode): NimNode {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc returnTo(scope: Scope): NimNode {...}{.raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
given a scope, find the ident|sym it's pointing to, or nil   Source   Edit 
proc newScope(n: NimNode; parent: Scope = nil): Scope {...}{.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc newScope(parent: NimNode; name: NimNode; n: NimNode): Scope {...}{.raises: [],
    tags: [].}
avoid returnTo() and form our chosen name   Source   Edit 
proc add(ns: var Scopes; k: NimNode; n: NimNode) {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
  Source   Edit 
proc openScope(s: Scope; n: NimNode): Scope {...}{.raises: [ValueError], tags: [].}
open a new scope for the given node; returns the current scope   Source   Edit 
proc closeScope(s: Scope; n: NimNode): Scope {...}{.raises: [], tags: [].}
close an open scope for the given node; returns the current scope   Source   Edit

Templates

template withScope(s: Scope; n: NimNode; body: untyped)
do some work on a particular node with a particular scope   Source   Edit
